steps.title: Find the grammatical parts around the blank
steps.content: The word before the blank is the subject 'he'. The word after the blank begins the direct object 'the sound'. A transitive verb needs no punctuation between it and its object, so nothing should appear in the blank.

steps.title: Apply the verb‑object rule
steps.content: Choice B 'preferred the' places the verb directly before its object with no punctuation, satisfying the rule. Choices A, C, and D each insert a semicolon, comma, or period that wrongly separates the verb from its object. That matches choice B — B is correct.
